data.title: AirSculpt revises annual revenue outlook
data.description:
AirSculpt Technologies (NASDAQ:AIRS) expects a 9% Y/Y decline in its third quarter revenue, with same store cases down 8.1% Y/Y.

Q3 revenue is expected to be approximately $42.5M, compared to analysts’ estimates of $41.64M.

“While we continue to see top-line pressures primarily from macro-economic challenges, we were pleased to see that our same store cases were much improved compared to the 14% decline we experienced in the second quarter,” said Dennis Dean, interim chief executive officer and chief financial officer of AirSculpt Technologies.

Based on the preliminary revenue results for Q3, AirSculpt (AIRS) revised its full-year revenue forecast to $183M-$189M as compared to its previous guidance of $180M-190M. The company is also maintaining its full year adjusted EBITDA outlook of $23M-$28M.

AirSculpt (AIRS) will announce its Q3 results on November 8, 2024.

data.title: Rogers beats Q3 top and bottom line estimates, reaffirms FY outlook
data.description:
• Rogers press release (NYSE:RCI): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.42 beats by $0.45.
  • Revenue of $5.13B (+0.8% Y/Y) beats by $1.38B.

  • Company reaffirms 2024 outlook: Total service revenue growth of 8% to 10%; adjusted EBITDA growth of 12% to 15%; capital expenditures of $3.8 billion to $4.0 billion; and free cash flow of $2.9 billion to $3.1 billion

data.title: LKQ authorizes $1B increase to stock buyback program
data.description:
• LKQ (NASDAQ:LKQ) said its board has authorized a $1 billion increase and a one-year extension to its stock repurchase program.
  • The aggregate authorization under the program has been raised to $4.5 billion and authorizing repurchases through October 25, 2026.

data.title: Las Vegas Sands gains as investors eye capital allocation moves
data.description:
Las Vegas Sands Corp. (NYSE:LVS) rose 1.7% in premarket trading on Thursday, despite the casino giant falling short of consensus estimates with its Q3 earnings report. Investors may have had their eyes on the capital allocation front, where the Las Vegas Sands (LVS) board of directors authorized $2.0 billion in future stock repurchases and announced a $0.20 increase in the recurring common stock dividend for the 2025 calendar year.

Jefferies analyst David Katz said lower-than-expected hold at Marina Bay Sands, disruptions created by renovations at The Londoner, as well as macroeconomic weakness in Macau highlighted the company's lower-than-expected Q3 performance. "We anticipate that as macro conditions improve and renovation-related disruptions decrease, the company could be better-positioned within Macau and Singapore," he added.

During the earnings call (transcript), CEO Robert Goldstein said the company believes that the Macau market gross gaming revenues will exceed $30 billion in 2025 and grow from there. He also pointed to considerable runway for growth in Singapore after LVS completes its investment programs in the region.

Shares of Las Vegas Sands (LVS) were up 1.67% in premarket trading to $51.89 vs. the 52-week range of $36.62 to $55.66. The Seeking Alpha Quant Rating on LVS is flashing Buy.
